title: santalum insulare var. marchionense
intro: Santalum insulare var. marchionense, also known as the Lord Howe Island sandalwood, is a rare and endangered variant of the Santalum insulare plant, a type of sandalwood native to the Lord Howe Island in Australia. This plant is valued for its unique and distinctive fragrance, which is extracted for use in perfumes, aromatherapy, and traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Santalum insulare var. marchionense include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat a range of health conditions, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of this plant include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and boosting the immune system, and it is often used in herbal preparations such as essential oils, salves, and tinctures.
